title: Phishing email reported by 5 users

servicenow_seed.short_description: [TRAINING] Phishing email reported by 5 users in same department
servicenow_seed.description: Five users in Finance reported the same suspicious email asking them to verify payroll details. One user clicked the link before reporting.

correct_steps: Verify reporter and tell every caller NOT to click, reply, or forward
Collect sender, subject, timestamp, headers (if available), URL, and attachment names
Check related incidents - confirm at least 5 reports
Identify the user who clicked: capture device, time, and any credentials entered
Link related tickets to a parent incident and escalate to Security Operations
rationale: A coordinated phishing wave is a SecOps event. Tier 1 collects evidence, contains the clicker, and hands off; Tier 1 does not investigate phishing alone.
hints: Tell users: do not click, do not reply, do not forward.
The user who clicked is your most urgent containment task.
learning_objectives: Treat multi-user phishing reports as a coordinated event
Contain the clicker before continuing the investigation

tool_clues.email: 5 messages from billing@payro11-update.com received between 09:12-09:14. 1 user clicked the URL.
tool_clues.security: URL flagged as 'credential phishing' by reputation feed.
